name: vercel
description: Deploy and manage Vercel projects, domains, environment variables, and serverless functions using the `vercel` CLI.
capabilities: Deploy current directory or production builds to Vercel
List and inspect deployments with status information
Manage Vercel projects and link local directories
Run local development server with vercel dev
author: Dicklesworthstone
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation skill containing only markdown documentation with example Vercel CLI commands. No executable code, scripts, or command execution is present. All static findings are false positives caused by the analyzer misidentifying documentation examples as code patterns. The skill is safe for publication.
valueStatement: Deploying frontend projects to Vercel requires navigating multiple CLI commands. This skill provides ready-to-use commands for deployments, domain management, environment variables, and local development without needing to reference external documentation.
userTitle: Deploy to Vercel from command line
limitations: Requires vercel CLI to be installed on the system
Does not create or configure Vercel accounts
Does not modify DNS settings directly

useCases.title: Deploy frontend applications
useCases.description: Quickly deploy React, Next.js, or static sites to Vercel with single commands

useCases.title: Manage deployment pipelines
useCases.description: List deployments, inspect logs, and manage environment variables across projects

useCases.title: Local development workflow
useCases.description: Run local development server and pull project settings for seamless iteration

promptTemplates.title: Basic deployment
promptTemplates.prompt: Deploy this project to Vercel using the vercel skill

promptTemplates.title: Production deployment
promptTemplates.prompt: Deploy this project to Vercel production using the vercel skill

promptTemplates.title: Check deployment status
promptTemplates.prompt: List recent Vercel deployments and show their status

promptTemplates.title: Environment management
promptTemplates.prompt: Pull environment variables from Vercel to local .env file

bestPractices: Run vercel whoami first to verify you are authenticated
Use vercel env pull to sync environment variables before local development
Test locally with vercel dev before deploying to production
antiPatterns: Deploying directly to production without testing on preview deployments
Hardcoding environment variables instead of using Vercel env management
Skipping deployment logs review after initial deployment

faq.question: Is the Vercel CLI required?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill provides commands for the Vercel CLI. Install it globally via npm: npm i -g vercel

faq.question: What can I deploy?
faq.answer: Vercel supports Next.js, React, Vue, Svelte, static sites, and serverless functions across many frameworks.

faq.question: How do I link a project?
faq.answer: Run vercel link to connect your local directory to an existing Vercel project.

faq.question: Is my data safe?
faq.answer: This skill only documents CLI commands. Actual deployment data stays within Vercel infrastructure.

faq.question: Why is deployment failing?
faq.answer: Common issues include authentication problems, missing build settings, or exceeded account limits. Check logs.

faq.question: How is this different from Vercel integrations?
faq.answer: This skill provides direct CLI access for workflows where you want full control over the deployment process.
